skill.name: Programmatic SEO
skill.description: Design and evaluate programmatic SEO strategies for creating SEO-driven pages at scale using templates and structured data. Use when the user mentions programmatic SEO, pages at scale, template pages, directory pages, location pages, comparison pages, integration pages, or keyword-pattern page generation. This skill focuses on feasibility, strategy, and page system design—not execution unless explicitly requested.
skill.summary: Strategic framework for evaluating and designing SEO-driven pages at scale with feasibility scoring and quality controls.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This is a prompt-only skill containing no executable code. The static analysis scanned 0 files with 0 lines of code and detected 0 security issues. The skill is a pure text-based prompt that provides SEO strategy guidance. No risky patterns were identified. This skill is safe for publication.

content.value_statement: Programmatic SEO can generate thousands of pages but risks thin content penalties if not done correctly. This skill evaluates feasibility and designs scalable page systems that avoid doorway pages and index bloat.
actual_capabilities: Calculate a 100-point Feasibility Index to determine if programmatic SEO is appropriate for a use case
Design page systems with proper URL architecture, data models, and template specifications
Identify and prevent risky patterns like doorway pages, thin content, and index bloat
Create indexation rules and crawl management strategies for large-scale page sets
Define quality gates and kill switch criteria to prevent algorithmic penalties
Match keyword patterns to appropriate playbook types (locations, comparisons, integrations, etc.)
limitations: Does not implement or execute programmatic SEO—only provides strategy and design
Does not guarantee search rankings or traffic increases
Does not provide actual SEO content—the skill designs systems, not pages
Cannot access real-time search data or competitor analytics

use_cases.title: Evaluate New Programmatic SEO Initiative
use_cases.description: Before building thousands of pages, score the feasibility of the project using the 100-point index. Get a clear go/no-go recommendation with risk factors identified.
use_cases.target_user: SEO managers and digital marketers evaluating new page-scale projects

use_cases.title: Design Page System Architecture
use_cases.description: Map out URL patterns, data models, template structures, and indexation rules for a programmatic SEO campaign. Ensure quality at scale from the start.
use_cases.target_user: Technical SEO specialists and web developers building page generators

use_cases.title: Audit Existing Programmatic Pages
use_cases.description: Review existing programmatic pages for quality issues, thin content risks, and indexation problems. Identify pages that should be de-indexed or improved.
use_cases.target_user: SEO auditors and agency consultants troubleshooting underperforming pages

prompt_templates.title: Basic Feasibility Check
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate whether programmatic SEO is a good fit for our [industry/niche] website targeting [keyword pattern]. We have access to [data source]. Help me calculate the Feasibility Index and determine if we should proceed.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick evaluation of a potential programmatic SEO project

prompt_templates.title: Full Strategy Design
prompt_templates.prompt: We want to create [number] pages for [keyword pattern]. Our product database has [data fields]. Design a complete page system including URL structure, data model, template sections, and indexation rules. Show the Feasibility Index score first.

prompt_templates.title: Risk Assessment
prompt_templates.prompt: Our programmatic SEO campaign has produced [number] pages but we're seeing [symptoms: low engagement, index bloat, thin content warnings]. Analyze what's wrong and recommend fixes including which pages to de-index.
prompt_templates.scenario: Diagnosing problems with an existing programmatic SEO implementation

prompt_templates.title: Playbook Selection
prompt_templates.prompt: We have data about [topic] including [specific data points]. Which of the 12 programmatic SEO playbooks fits best? Show how the data maps to page templates and what unique value each page would provide.
prompt_templates.scenario: Choosing the right programmatic SEO approach for available data

output_examples.input: Evaluate programmatic SEO for a real estate site creating pages for every city and neighborhood in the US.
output_examples.output:
Feasibility Index: 82/100 - Strong Fit

**Category Breakdown:**
- Search Pattern Validity: 18/20 - Clear geographic intent
- Unique Value per Page: 22/25 - Local data, market stats, neighborhood details
- Data Availability: 16/20 - MLS data accessible
- Search Intent Alignment: 14/15 - Strong local search intent
- Competitive Feasibility: 6/10 - Competitive but beatable
- Operational Sustainability: 6/10 - Regular data updates needed

**Verdict: Strong Fit** - Proceed with geographic playbook.

**Recommended Approach:** Location-based playbook with city profiles, neighborhood guides, and market trend pages. Prioritize cities with sufficient search volume.

output_examples.input: Design page system for software comparison pages comparing [tool A] vs [tool B] for every software category.
output_examples.output:
Feasibility Index: 71/100 - Moderate Fit

**Key Considerations:**
- Focus on high-intent comparison queries
- Include unique data: pricing, features, user ratings
- Limit to categories with commercial intent
- Build comparison matrix as unique value driver

**URL Pattern:** /compare/[category]/[tool-a]-vs-[tool-b]

**Indexation Rule:** Only index if both tools have sufficient search volume and unique differentiation exists.

best_practices: Always calculate the Feasibility Index first—do not proceed with scores below 65 without strong controls
Every page must answer 'why does this page deserve to exist separately?' with a clear unique value proposition
Implement indexation rules before generating pages—only index pages with demonstrated demand and complete intent match
anti_patterns: Creating pages that differ only by swapped keywords without meaningful unique content
Generating pages for every possible permutation regardless of search demand
Indexing all generated pages without quality gates—this leads to thin content penalties and index bloat

faq.question: What is the Feasibility Index?
faq.answer: The Feasibility Index is a 100-point scoring system that evaluates whether programmatic SEO is appropriate for a specific use case. Scores above 80 indicate strong fit, 65-79 moderate fit, 50-64 high risk, and below 50 means do not proceed.

faq.question: Does this skill create actual pages?
faq.answer: No. This skill provides strategic guidance and design recommendations only. It does not implement or execute programmatic SEO—you will need other skills or developers to build the pages.

faq.question: What are the 12 playbooks?
faq.answer: The 12 programmatic SEO playbooks are: Templates, Curation, Conversions, Comparisons, Examples, Locations, Personas, Integrations, Glossary, Translations, Directories, and Profiles.

faq.question: How do I avoid doorway page penalties?
faq.answer: Avoid doorway pages by ensuring every page has unique value beyond swapped variables, fully satisfies search intent, and provides information users would expect to find. The skill's quality gates help prevent this.

faq.question: What data sources work best for programmatic SEO?
faq.answer: Proprietary data is strongest, followed by product-derived data, user-generated content, licensed exclusive data, and finally public data (weakest). Stronger data requires less editorial effort.

faq.question: Can this help fix existing SEO problems?
faq.answer: Yes. You can use the skill to audit existing programmatic pages, identify thin content or indexing issues, and recommend which pages should be improved or de-indexed.
